learning_outcomes.description: sketch, interpret and manipulate functions of two or more variables.
calculate the equations of tangent planes, and use them to approximate functions.
find the maxima and minima of functions of two variables.
apply the method of Lagrange multipliers to optimisation problems.
solve and analyse certain families of first and second order ODEs.
model a problem with ODEs and either analytically or numerically solve the differential equation and interpret the output.
work fluently with parametric forms of curves, and derive parametric forms.
understand fields and determine conservative fields and path integrals.
model and solve problems with some real-world complexity.

assessments.weighting: <dd>15% Each exercise is marked out of 5. The best 8 marks will count for assessment. </dd>
assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description: You will complete an exercise at the applied class each week, except in Weeks 1, 4 and 11.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The exercise is completed within the scheduled applied class time and turned in in-person at the end of the applied class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: As noted under Extensions, assessment is completed within the scheduled class time and turned in at the end of the applied class. There are no late submissions as assessments are completed in class. Instead, students will drop the lowest two applied class assessment scores to account for absences due to illness or exceptional circumstances.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: You must submit detailed written solutions to a collection of mathematical problems.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via Gradescope. Instructions are on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
In-semester Saturday

5/09/2026 - 20/09/2026

You must hold all of the following Friday (evening only), Saturday and Sunday dates free until the exam date is officially announced at 5pm on August 6th:

  • 5 September
  • 11-13 September
  • 18-20 September
assessments.task_description:
The in-semester examination will be an in-person, paper-based exam. Students will sit the exam at the St Lucia Campus.

There will be a range of problem solving questions.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: The end-of-semester exam will be an in-person, paper-based exam. Students will sit the exam at the St Lucia Campus.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Applied Class

In the applied classes students will complete collaborative exercises and receive help on course work from the Casual Academic.
learning_activities.topic_description: Applied Class In the applied classes students will complete collaborative exercises and receive help on course work from the Casual Academic.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

Lectures will be given in person. Topics include:
Functions of several variables;
Partial derivatives and tangent planes;
Maximum and minimum problems on surfaces;
Differential Equations;
Parametrisation of curves and line integrals.
learning_activities.topic_description: Lectures Lectures will be given in person. Topics include: Functions of several variables; Partial derivatives and tangent planes; Maximum and minimum problems on surfaces; Differential Equations; Parametrisation of curves and line integrals.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Exam week 2(27 Jul - 22 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Independent work

During the semester students are expected to work on MATH1052 outside of class time to consolidate the information given during lectures, work on applied class materials and assignments, complete extra practice problems and study for examinations.
learning_activities.topic_description: Independent work During the semester students are expected to work on MATH1052 outside of class time to consolidate the information given during lectures, work on applied class materials and assignments, complete extra practice problems and study for examinations.
